questions.question: TrueHarvest Co. can no longer supply ingredients. What is the impact?
questions.answer: TrueHarvest Co. supplies 3 key ingredients used in 4 products across 2 plants. Disruption would affect retail and DTC channels, impacting 3 major store accounts. Alternative sourcing should be prioritized for Mango Puree (single-source) and Fresh Tomatoes (FreshFarm Co. as backup).
reasoningSteps: TrueHarvest Co. supplies: Mango Puree, Apple Puree, Fresh Tomatoes
Products affected: Mango Chutney, Apple Cider Vinegar, Tomato Ketchup, Pesto Basilico
Plants impacted: East Coast Production Facility, Midwest Processing Plant, West Coast Bottling
Channels at risk: Retail Stores, E-Commerce DTC
Stores affected: Whole Foods, Target, Mango Saree

questions.question: Which suppliers and ingredients go into Mango Chutney?
questions.answer: Mango Chutney depends on 4 ingredients from 3 suppliers. Mango Puree from TrueHarvest Co. is the most critical - it is single-sourced. The product is manufactured at the East Coast facility and distributed through retail and DTC channels.
reasoningSteps: Mango Chutney uses: Mango Puree, Cane Sugar, Citric Acid, Cayenne Pepper
Supplied by: TrueHarvest Co. (Mango Puree), Natural Flavors Co. (Citric Acid), Kroger Ingredients (Cane Sugar, Cayenne Pepper)
Manufactured at: East Coast Production Facility
Sold through: Retail Stores, E-Commerce DTC

questions.question: If we lose the Whole Foods account, which products are most affected?
questions.answer: Whole Foods carries 5 of our 10 products. Mango Chutney and Pesto Basilico have the highest concentration risk, with 40% and 35% of retail volume respectively flowing through this account. Garlic Aioli and Honey Mustard have better diversification across Target and regional chains.
reasoningSteps: Whole Foods carries: Mango Chutney, Garlic Aioli, Honey Mustard, Pesto Basilico, Apple Cider Vinegar, Sourdough Bread
Of these, Mango Chutney and Pesto Basilico have Whole Foods as their largest retail account
Garlic Aioli and Honey Mustard are also in Target and Chicago Outlet
Revenue concentration risk is highest for Mango Chutney (40% of retail volume through Whole Foods)

questions.question: East Coast Production Facility goes offline. What is affected?
questions.answer: The East Coast facility produces 4 products serving all major channels and 4 store accounts. Hot Sauce and Tomato Ketchup can be rerouted to the Midwest Plant. Mango Chutney and Garlic Aioli have no alternative production facility - these would face supply interruption until the facility is restored.
reasoningSteps: East Coast produces: Mango Chutney, Tomato Ketchup, Hot Sauce 150ml, Garlic Aioli
These products are sold through: Retail Stores, E-Commerce DTC, Wholesale/B2B, Foodservice
Stores affected: Whole Foods, Target, Mango Saree, Chicago Outlet
Midwest Processing Plant could absorb Hot Sauce and Tomato Ketchup production
Mango Chutney and Garlic Aioli have no alternative plant - production would halt
